summary: High‑energy San Diego show with standout High Time, Minglewood, Sugaree and a strong Estimated Prophet solo; audience recording is clear enough despite some oversaturation.
review: The concert delivers an inspired night – Jerry’s vocals are surprisingly solid through Black Peter, Brent shines on Estimated Prophet, and the band rolls out memorable versions of High Time, Minglewood, Sugaree and Stagger Lee. The audience‑sourced tape is generally good, though a touch of oversaturation and flat‑tone quirks required some post‑processing.

ai_rating.rationale: Four reviews (three 5‑star, one 3‑star) overwhelmingly praise the performance and note only minor recording flaws; weighted blend of show (70%) and recording (30%) yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Flat treble corrected with added EQ, some oversaturation on two tracks, occasional reverb added to compensate for stark audience ambience; overall listenable and fairly clear.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows smoothly, opening with tight energy and building to an expansive 15‑minute Sugaree; the second half maintains momentum with strong transitions and a jazzy Estimated Prophet solo.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals are notably solid throughout, especially on Black Peter—a relief after earlier 1979 vocal issues.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent steps up on Estimated Prophet, delivering a jazz‑inflected solo that exceeds his usual low‑brow approach.
